channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Three women found dead off UK Brighton Beach identified as sisters
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Children across England to get free bus travel this August under £100 million Government Scheme
item.description: Millions of children aged five to 15 in England will receive free local bus travel throughout August as part of a government initiative to ease the cost of living. This "Great British Summer Savings" package aims to help families with rising expenses during the school holidays, potentially saving households significant amounts.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists turn sheep wool into a bone-healing material in a major medical breakthrough
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: London heatwave set to hit 29C temperatures this weekend as UK turns warmer than Athens and Ibiza
item.description: London and southern England are set for a significant heatwave this Bank Holiday weekend. Temperatures are forecast to reach 29C, potentially exceeding popular Mediterranean destinations. This warm spell follows a period of cold and wet weather. Health officials have issued alerts advising caution for vulnerable groups. This could be one of the warmest May Bank Holiday weekends in years.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists decode 11,000-year-old DNA and discover Britain’s oldest human was a three-year-old girl
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by German philosopher Immanuel Kant: “When I could have used a wife, I could not support one; and when I could…”
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Mystery substance scare: 3 dead, first responders quarantined over toxic exposure in US
item.description: A suspected drug overdose in rural New Mexico turned deadly, claiming three lives and exposing over a dozen first responders to an unknown substance. Emergency personnel experienced severe symptoms like nausea and dizziness, leading to widespread decontamination efforts.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Another military operation on cards? USS Nimitz enters Caribbean amid US-Cuba tensions
item.description: Amidst escalating US-Cuba tensions, the USS Nimitz strike group has entered the Caribbean. This follows President Trump's intensified rhetoric and a recent indictment of former Cuban President Raul Castro. The US is increasing pressure on Havana, citing economic hardship and past incidents, though Trump downplays fears of direct military action.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: ‘Hair on fire’: Trump, Netanyahu call turns tense over Iran strategy
item.description: US President Trump and Israeli PM Netanyahu clashed over Iran diplomacy, with Trump favoring a deal and Netanyahu pushing for military action. Mediators from Qatar, Pakistan, and others are working on a revised proposal, but Trump warns the window for talks is closing, while Israel remains on high alert.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: "Charlie was about to update his will": Candace Owens sparks backlash after explosive Charlie Kirk conspiracy claims on livestream
item.description: Candace Owens sparked major backlash after a controversial livestream filled with conspiracy claims surrounding Charlie Kirk, criticism of Victor Marx, and comments about Joe Rogan and Theo Von. Owens claimed “something isn’t adding up” regarding Kirk’s death and suggested hidden motives tied to his will and communications. Critics on X accused her of pushing conspiracies without evidence, calling the broadcast chaotic and misleading.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Was Nina Lin arrested? Twitch streamer claims missed $4 scissors scan led to night in jail
item.description: Nina Lin revealed on Twitch that she allegedly spent a night in jail after failing to scan a $4 pair of scissors during self checkout. The streamer claimed it was an honest mistake caused by rushing through checkout, but store staff still involved police. The incident reignited debate online because Nina previously faced similar shoplifting accusations in 2025. She also suggested critics linked to Adin Ross and Asmongold may have influenced the situation.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Tennessee man jailed for 37 days over Charlie Kirk meme to receive $835,000 settlement
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'There won't be escalation': Donald Trump reacts to Raul Castro's indictment by DOJ
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Will Raúl Castro’s indictment push US and Cuba closer to war?
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Israeli parliament votes to advance bill to dissolve Knesset, paving way for early elections
item.description: Israeli lawmakers overwhelmingly advanced a bill to dissolve parliament, potentially triggering early elections and intensifying political uncertainty for Prime Minister Netanyahu. This move stems from growing coalition strains, particularly over military service exemptions for ultra-Orthodox parties. Opposition leaders have declared the election campaign has begun, anticipating a closely contested vote.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Failed 3 H1-B attempts: Indian working in US says ‘visa anxiety is a permanent subscription’
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists grow a bacteria-made “living” supermaterial that could replace plastic
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: David Lammy unveils reforms to reduce youth jail terms and protect vulnerable children in the UK
item.description: Justice Secretary David Lammy unveiled plans to cut youth custody by 25%, inspired by his own experiences growing up in Tottenham. The reforms aim to reduce pre-trial detentions, introduce youth intervention courts, and enhance parental accountability. These measures seek to divert children from crime and prevent future offending by offering community sentences and specialized support.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Indian realtor in NZ says neighbour urinated on fence: 'My family and I have...'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: World Bee Day campaign encourages public to protect declining UK pollinators
item.description: The RSPCA urges public support for declining bee populations, with a third lost in three decades, impacting vital crops. Initiatives like the South Downs' Bee Lines project create wildflower habitats. Experts highlight bees' intelligence and complex lives, emphasizing simple actions like providing water and planting native flowers can significantly aid their survival and welfare.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
